_meta.full_name: IPIP-HEXACO Scales — International Personality Item Pool representation of the HEXACO personality model (Ashton, Lee, & Goldberg, 2007)
_meta.license: Public domain. Per the official IPIP website (ipip.ori.org): "The items and scales are in the public domain, which means that one can copy, edit, translate, or use them for any purpose without asking permission and without paying a fee." The IPIP was created by Lewis R. Goldberg with NIMH support; no permission or fee is required for any use, commercial or non-commercial.
_meta.attribution: Items authored/compiled by Lewis R. Goldberg and the International Personality Item Pool (IPIP; https://ipip.ori.org). Scale construction: Ashton, M. C., Lee, K., & Goldberg, L. R. (2007). The IPIP-HEXACO scales: An alternative, public-domain measure of the personality constructs in the HEXACO model. Personality and Individual Differences, 42, 1515-1526. The IPIP representations were built to parallel the constructs of Lee & Ashton's (2004) HEXACO Personality Inventory. Recommended IPIP citation: Goldberg, L. R. (1999) and Goldberg et al. (2006), Journal of Research in Personality, 40, 84-96.
_meta.scoring_notes: Structure: 6 HEXACO factors, each with 4 facet scales (24 facets total), each facet measured by exactly 10 items = 240 items. Factor -> facet map with IPIP abbreviations and Cronbach's alpha reported on the source key page (from Ashton, Lee, & Goldberg, 2007): HONESTY-HUMILITY {Sincerity (H:Sinc) a=.81, Fairness (H:Fair) a=.77, Greed Avoidance (H:Gree) a=.69, Modesty (H:Mode) a=.81}; EMOTIONALITY {Fearfulness (E:Fear) a=.84, Anxiety (E:Anxi) a=.85, Dependence (E:Depe) a=.73 (all 10 items +keyed), Sentimentality (E:Sent) a=.79}; EXTRAVERSION {Expressiveness (X:Expr) a=.84, Social Boldness (X:SocB) a=.86, Sociability (X:Soci) a=.85, Liveliness (X:Live) a=.82}; AGREEABLENESS {Forgiveness (A:Forg) a=.78, Gentleness (A:Gent) a=.81, Flexibility (A:Flex) a=.73, Patience (A:Pati) a=.88}; CONSCIENTIOUSNESS {Organization (C:Orga) a=.85, Diligence (C:Dili) a=.81, Perfectionism (C:Perf) a=.80, Prudence (C:Prud) a=.80}; OPENNESS TO EXPERIENCE {Aesthetic Appreciation (O:AesA) a=.83, Inquisitiveness (O:Inqu) a=.78, Creativity (O:Crea) a=.85, Unconventionality (O:Unco) a=.84}. Keying: keyed="+" items score in the direction of the facet; keyed="-" items are reverse-scored before summing. On the customary 5-point scale (1=Very Inaccurate ... 5=Very Accurate) reverse scoring is 5->1, 4->2, 3->3, 2->4, 1->5. Overall balance across the 240 items: 118 positively keyed, 122 negatively keyed. Items are first-person self-report statements administered with a standard IPIP instruction stem ("Describe yourself as you generally are now... indicate how accurately each statement describes you").
_meta.cautions: 1) This is the FULL 240-item facet-level IPIP-HEXACO (24 facet scales x 10 items), fetched verbatim from the official IPIP key page. The task cited "the 100-item IPIP-HEXACO" only as an example; the authoritative IPIP site hosts this complete 240-item facet version, so the entire coherent set was collected rather than a partial subset. All 240 items were captured (none omitted); a shorter 100-item or 60-item public-domain HEXACO subset can be derived from these same items if needed. 2) The "num" field is the sequential position within the source key listing (ordered factor -> facet -> +keyed then -keyed), NOT an official IPIP master-item identifier; IPIP assigns its own item IDs in its 3,000+ alphabetical item pool. 3) These public-domain IPIP items are an ALTERNATIVE proxy for the HEXACO constructs; they are NOT the copyrighted HEXACO-PI-R by Lee & Ashton. The proprietary HEXACO-PI-R (hosted at hexaco.org) was deliberately NOT reproduced here; only the public-domain IPIP representation was collected. 4) Reported alpha reliabilities are from the 2007 development sample and are provided on the source page for reference. 5) Item text is reproduced exactly as it appears on the IPIP key page (including punctuation and quotation marks); these are stage-1 raw items intended to be rewritten into plain language downstream.

verification.summary: adversarially fact-checked by a second agent vs primary sources (deepinme wide-collection workflow, 2026-07)

sources.title: IPIP-HEXACO key page: The Items in the Preliminary IPIP Scales Measuring Constructs Similar to Those Included in Lee and Ashton's HEXACO Personality Inventory (official IPIP / Oregon Research Institute)
International Personality Item Pool (IPIP) home page — public-domain statement and site overview

Ashton, Lee, & Goldberg (2007), The IPIP-HEXACO scales: An alternative, public-domain measure of the personality constructs in the HEXACO model, Personality and Individual Differences, 42, 1515-1526 (author PDF)

items.text: Don't pretend to be more than I am.
Tell other people what they want to hear so that they will do what I want them to do.
Put on a show to impress people.
Switch my loyalties when I feel like it.
Play a role in order to impress people.
Pretend to be concerned for others.
Act like different people in different situations.
Find it necessary to please the people who have power.
Let people push me around to help them feel important.
Would never take things that aren't mine.
Would never cheat on my taxes.
Return extra change when a cashier makes a mistake.
Would feel very badly for a long time if I were to steal from someone.
Cheat on people who have trusted me.
Would not regret my behavior if I were to take advantage of someone impulsively.
Would not enjoy being a famous celebrity.
Don't strive for elegance in my appearance.
Have a strong need for power.
Prefer to eat at expensive restaurants.
Am out for my own personal gain.
Don't think that I'm better than other people.
See myself as an average person.
Would like to have more power than other people.
Believe that I am better than others.
Am more capable than most others.
Am likely to show off if I get the chance.
Begin to panic when there is danger.
Would fear walking in a high-crime part of a city.
Would never go riding down a stretch of rapids in a canoe.
Would be good at rescuing people from a burning building.
Often worry about things that turn out to be unimportant.
Get upset by unpleasant thoughts that come into my mind.
Am not easily disturbed by events.
Don't worry about things that have already happened.
Let myself be influenced by others.
Suspect that my facial expressions give me away when I feel sad.
Can't do without the company of others.
Immediately feel sad when hearing of an unhappy event.
Am sensitive to the needs of others.
Am deeply moved by others' misfortunes.
Seldom feel weepy while reading the sad part of a story.
Am seldom bothered by the apparent suffering of strangers.
Don't understand people who get emotional.
Am never at a loss for words.
Am the life of the party.
Tell people about it when I'm irritated.
Don't like to draw attention to myself.
Am good at making impromptu speeches.
Don't mind being the center of attention.
Would be afraid to give a speech in public.
Find it difficult to approach others.
Hate being the center of attention.
Usually like to spend my free time with people.
Talk to a lot of different people at parties.
Enjoy being part of a group.
Seem to derive less enjoyment from interacting with people than others do.
Would not enjoy a job that involves a lot of social interaction.
Am hard to get to know.
Maintain high energy throughout the day.
Am usually active and full of energy.
Feel healthy and vibrant most of the time.
Feel that I have a lot of inner strength.
Am nice to people I should be angry at.
Find it hard to forgive others.
Get back at people who insult me.
Feel that most people can't be trusted.
Have a good word for everyone.
Become frustrated and angry with people when they don't live up to my expectations.
When interacting with a group of people, am often bothered by at least one of them.
Get upset if others change the way that I have arranged things.
Find that it takes a lot to make me feel angry at someone.
Leave a mess in my room.
Don't finish the things that I start.
Often forget to put things back in their proper place.
Push myself very hard to succeed.
Get started quickly on doing a job.
Do just enough work to get by.
Stop when work becomes too difficult.
Quickly lose interest in the tasks I start.
Want every detail taken care of.
Want everything to add up perfectly.
Pay too little attention to details.
Prefer to just let things happen.
Make plans and stick to them.
Do things according to a plan.
Do things without thinking of the consequences.
Act impulsively when something is bothering me.
Don't know why I do some of the things I do.
Believe in the importance of art.
See beauty in things that others might not notice.
Have read the great literary classics.
Seldom notice the emotional aspects of paintings and pictures.
Do not enjoy watching dance performances.
Would love to explore strange places.
Don't bother worrying about political and social problems.
Will not probe deeply into a subject.
Carry the conversation to a higher level.
Love to think up new ways of doing things.
Do not have a good imagination.
Have trouble guessing how others will react.
Am considered to be kind of eccentric.
Know that my ideas sometimes surprise people.
Do things that others find strange.
Would hate to be considered odd or strange.
Enjoy being thought of as a normal "mainstream" person.
Like to be viewed as proper and conventional.
Like to be thought of as a normal kind of person.
